1.description: (Color online) Layouts for Each Experiment Source . Reutskaja et al. (2011) , figure reconstructed for increased resolution. Copyright American Economic Association; reproduced with permission of the American Economic Review. Notes . For Experiments 1 and 2, the solid lines delineate product AOIs, and the dotted lines delineate the category AOI. In Experiment 3, participants were shown grids of snack foods ranging in size from 4 to 16. (a) Experiment 1. (b) Experiment 2. (c) Experiment 3.

2.description: Overview of the Two-Stage Decision Process for Fixation Events

3.description: Experiment 1 Observed vs. Simulated Total Fixations and Clicked AOI Attractiveness for the Full Model, (a) and (e), vs. Benchmark Models, (b)–(d) and (f)–(h) Notes . The dotted line in each plot is the identity line, and the solid line in each plot represents the regression line through the points, with each point representing one shopper. (a) Full model: total fixations. (b) Static threshold: total fixations. (c) Stochastic accumulator: total fixations. (d) Population threshold: total fixations. (e) Full model: attractiveness. (f) Static threshold: attractiveness. (g) Stochastic accumulator: attractiveness. (h) Population threshold: attractiveness.

4.description: Experiment 2 Observed vs. Simulated Total Fixations and Clicked AOI Attractiveness for the Full Model, (a) and (e), vs. Benchmark Models, (b)–(d) and (f)–(h) Notes . Identity lines are dotted, and regression lines are solid, with each point representing one shopper. (a) Full model: total fixations. (b) Static threshold: total fixations. (c) Stochastic accumulator: total fixations. (d) Population threshold: total fixations. (e) Full model: attractiveness. (f) Static threshold: attractiveness. (g) Stochastic accumulator: attractiveness. (h) Population threshold: attractiveness.

5.description: Experiment 3 Observed vs. Simulated Total Fixations and Clicked AOI Attractiveness for the Full Model, (a) and (d), vs. Benchmark Models, (b)–(c) and (e)–(f) Notes . (a) Full model: total fixations. (b) Static threshold: total fixations. (c) Stochastic accumulator: total fixations. (d) Full model: attractiveness. (e) Static threshold: attractiveness. (f) Stochastic accumulator: attractiveness.

6.description: Experiment 3 Counterfactual Comparisons for Worst-First vs. Best-First Web-Page Layouts Notes . Differences across measures computed by taking worst-first layout counterfactual simulation metrics and subtracting best-first layout metrics. (a) Total fixations (four choices). (b) Unique AOIs (four choices). (c) Clicked AOI liking (four choices). (d) Total fixations (nine choices). (e) Unique AOIs (nine choices). (f) Clicked AOI liking (nine choices). (g) Total fixations (16 choices). (h) Unique AOIs (16 choices). (i) Clicked AOI liking (16 choices).
